summary: Wrote integrated content + design reference at docs/workflows/content-standards.md pairing visible-component choice with copy shape: CTA capitalization (observed Title Case for buttons), error message phrasing (required resolution-guidance shape; current lastError templates flagged as not yet conforming), header / section-label punctuation (no terminal punctuation on labels, periods only on full sentences), and a component-to-content-type mapping table covering Button/Label/Section/Text/.navigationTitle/.alert/.confirmationDialog/.accessibilityLabel/.accessibilityHint/Continue-subtitle/digest-insight. Cross-linked the L() key-naming convention to localization-string-inventory.md rather than duplicating. Added a docs/README.md entry under the localization workflow links. No code, copy, or translation changes — the reference describes observed practice and names gaps for the queued audit slice to handle.
gaps_named_for_follow_up: All lastError templates in TrainStore/WriteStore/HomeStore/CareerStore/TodayStore/PatternStore are 'Failed to <verb> <noun>: \(error.localizedDescription)' shape — not localized, no resolution guidance. app-error-message-audit (queued pri 75) inventories the surfaces; per-store fix slices queued by that audit will replace the templates.
not_claimed: Existing copy has been rewritten to match the convention (the doc describes observed practice, with one named gap on error templates).
The error-template gap will be fixed automatically — the audit slice still needs to run and queue per-store fix slices.
Native-locale conventions match English Title Case (the doc explicitly states locale-specific capitalization rules apply for non-English).
The component-to-content-type mapping table is exhaustive — it covers the components seen in current Owlory views, not every SwiftUI component.
residual_risks: If a future component is introduced that does not match an existing row in the mapping table, the convention for it is undefined until the table is extended.
The 'no terminal punctuation on labels, periods on full sentences' rule is observed but not enforced by any tool. A small inconsistency could land and not be caught until a reviewer notices.
recommended_next_reason: Lowest priority among queued slices (pri 75); also the natural follow-up since this reference names the lastError templates as the most visible gap.
